what_it_does: Acceptance tests for the 'saving side' of chains/graphs promoted to named, LLM-configurable actions. It exercises build_action validation, ActionRegistry save/get/list/reload, attaching a calibrated config to an action, and build_coherence_info as a read-only projection of structural findings.
observations: Defines a global PASS counter and a check(label, cond) helper that asserts and tallies passes.
Hardcodes a test model set MODELS = {'qwen3.5-4b', 'bge-m3'} representing chat and embedding registry models.
Tests build_action accepts valid registry-referenced steps and rejects hardcoded model names and empty step lists.
Creates a temporary ActionRegistry, saves an action, reloads it, and verifies persistence.
Saves a calibration record under the action name and checks it is returned with the action.
Uses FsStore and coherence_detect.record_structural_findings to populate findings, then calls act.build_coherence_info.
Verifies build_coherence_info returns burn_down, finding_kinds, and dispositions, with 'unwired-route' appearing among derived kinds.
Checks build_coherence_info does not write by comparing store.all_findings() length before and after.
standouts: Fail-loud enforcement that action step models must be registry references, not hardcoded literals.
Calibration is treated as part of the saved action record.
build_coherence_info is explicitly tested as a read-only projection (reflects-never-owns).
conventions: Acceptance-test style with inline assertions rather than a unittest/pytest class.
Docstring uses lettered feature codes (E1, E2, D4, E4) to map tests to design concepts.
Uses sys.path.insert to locate the project root and imports runtime modules directly.
Uses tempfile.mkdtemp for isolated registry and store paths.
concerns: Global mutable PASS counter and side-effecting check() make the test harness less composable.
The available model set is hardcoded in the test and could drift from the real registry.
No cleanup of temporary directories is shown.
The test depends on runtime modules whose behavior is not visible in this file.
notes: An acceptance test that locks down declaration validation, registry persistence, calibration attachment, and read-only coherence projection for the action-saving layer.
questions: What do runtime.coherence_actions.build_action and ActionRegistry actually implement?
What does runtime.coherence_detect.record_structural_findings record and how are dispositions defined?
Is 'qwen3.5-4b' a real registered model or a placeholder?
What is the intended 'convergence-round seam' for E5 compose/run across models and embeddings?
Are there corresponding tests for the runner side (E3/E5) that this file deliberately excludes?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it validates and persists action declarations, attaches calibrations, and projects coherence info without writing.
intention_signals: To prove that action declarations have a single validation door through the registry, that saved actions carry proven calibration, and that coherence information is projected from an underlying store rather than owned by the projection layer.
novelty: It treats chains/graphs as first-class, named, registry-bound actions with attached calibration, and separately tests a read-only coherence-info projection as a sibling to a cognition write layer.
contribution: Provides acceptance tests that enforce registry-referenced model validation, verify action registry persistence and reload, verify calibration is stored with an action, and verify build_coherence_info is a read-only projection of existing findings.
summary_for_embedding: An acceptance test file that validates action declaration rules, action registry persistence and reload, calibration attachment, and read-only coherence-info projection for a chains-as-actions saving layer.
intention_for_embedding: To demonstrate and lock the saving-side contracts of configurable actions: registry-only model references, persistent registry rows, calibrated configs, and a non-mutating coherence read model.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that increments a global pass counter and prints an 'ok' line for each passing check.
symbols.description: The check(label, cond) function is the test harness used throughout the file: it asserts cond with a labeled failure message, increments the global PASS counter on success, and prints a per-check confirmation. It centralizes pass/fail tallying for the acceptance test.
